title: ononis trifoliolata
intro: Ononis trifoliolata, also known as the restharrow or devil's needle, is a perennial plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, characterized by its three-parted leaves and clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of glycosides and flavonoids. Consuming restharrow tea or decoction may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and may also provide relief from menstrual cramps and other menstrual-related ailments. Restharrow can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
